new_classes.definition_en: A material entity (plant material or aromatic substance) used to flavour wine of fresh grapes, as required for classification under CN heading 2205.

new_classes.label_en: Vermouth and other wine of fresh grapes, flavoured with plants or aromatic substances (heading 2205)
new_classes.label_de: Wermutwein und andere Weine aus frischen Weintrauben, mit Pflanzen oder anderen Stoffen aromatisiert (Position 2205)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes (including fortified wine) that has been flavoured with plants or aromatic substances, classified under CN heading 2205. Includes beverages such as Marsala all'uovo, Marsala alla mandorla, and Crema di Marsala all'uovo based on Marsala wine and flavoured with egg yolks or almonds.

new_classes.label_de: Mit Pflanzen oder aromatischen Stoffen aromatisiert – Eigenschaft
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a wine of fresh grapes indicating that it has been flavoured with plants or aromatic substances, distinguishing products of heading 2205 from unmodified wines of heading 2204.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) the product is a wine of fresh grapes (linked via subClassOf VermouthWineFreshGrapesFlavouredPlants2205), (2) it bears the quality of being flavoured with plants or aromatic substances, and (3) it has a flavouring substance as a part. A disjointness restriction from unmodified wine (WineOfFreshGrapes2204) is included. The specific named examples from the CN notes (Marsala all'uovo, Marsala alla mandorla, Crema di Marsala all'uovo with egg yolks/almonds) are documented in the class definition but cannot be fully formalised as OWL class restrictions without introducing highly specific ingredient classes (egg yolk, almond) whose scope would go beyond the current TBox. The coverage is therefore reduced to 0.8 to reflect this gap in formal axiomatisation of the specific product examples.
